items.title: Man jailed over manslaughter of surfer Guy Haymes in Manly apartment

items.summary: Some young Australians locked out of the property market due to unaffordable housing have pursued ETFs and "rentvesting" as a way to build financial security. Here's what they make of the budget's CGT reforms.

items.title: Should the tax system reward those who can afford to take risks?
items.summary: As the federal budget prompts howls of protest from those who earn the bulk of their income from owning assets rather than working, we shouldn't forget the relative privilege that has allowed many of those people to take those risks.

items.title: How to deal with money stress in your relationship
items.summary: While financial tension between partners isn't always about money, many people clash over values, spending habits and how they respond to financial pressure.

items.title: The Talented Mr F follows two filmmakers trying to find their stolen movie
items.summary: What do you do when your stolen film wins a slew of awards for the thief? In the case of these filmmakers, you travel across the globe to hunt him down.

items.title: Australians share the moment they realised gambling had changed their lives forever
items.summary: Australians are the world's biggest gamblers, losing billions every year. But the true cost goes far beyond the financial.

items.title: ABC Timelines quiz: Guess the correct order of these famous movie quotes from the 2000s
items.summary: The ABC Timelines quiz is back to test your knowledge of some of the most iconic movie quotes of the 2000s.

items.title: Elon Musk loses lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman
items.summary: The Tesla boss accused OpenAI of wrongfully trying to enrich investors and insiders at the nonprofit's expense, and failing to prioritise AI's safety.

items.title: Two Americans arrested over man breaking into enclosure of Punch the monkey
items.summary: One of the men was detained by a zookeeper and arrested after allegedly climbing a fence and breaking into the enclosure.

items.title: Iran International journalist Pouria Zaratifoukolaei stabbed in London at behest of Iran, court told
items.summary: Pouria Zaratifoukolaei, who works for a Persian-language outlet critical of the Iranian government, was attacked near his home in London in 2024.

items.title: Spanish PM's Socialists party on notice after historic defeat in stronghold
items.summary: Voters in the region of Andalusia have sent the ruling Spain's Socialists yet another warning, adding to concerns Pedro Sánchez's government could suffer an electoral wipe-out in a nationwide vote.

items.title: Man charged with murder after woman and two young children found dead in south-west Sydney home
items.summary: A man, 47, has been charged after three people were found dead in a home in Sydney's south-west in what police allege is a domestic violence-related incident.

items.title: Trial begins as WA police assistant commissioner Gailene Hamilton accused of stalking subordinate officer
items.summary: A WA police assistant commissioner faces trial accused of stalking a female subordinate officer and using a restricted computer system to track her movements.

items.title: NT police officer on trial charged with attempting to pervert the course of justice
items.summary: A jury has heard police officer Karol Jarentowski repeatedly attempted to pressure his former partner into dropping aggravated assault charges against him, including at one point threatening her child support.

items.title: Should we be worried about the latest Ebola outbreak?
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Super Netball Round-Up: From retirement to record breaker, Romelda Aiken-George keeps reinventing herself
items.summary: Romelda Aiken-George is supposed to be retired. Instead, the 37-year-old goal shooter is breaking all kinds of records and reinventing herself in her 19th season. After her 255th national league game on the weekend, she reveals the secret to her longevity.

items.title: Clare Wright wins top prize at NSW Literary Awards for 'powerful' history book Näku Dhäruk The Bark Petitions
items.summary: The 1963 Näku Dhäruk or Yirrkala bark petitions led to Australia's first land rights law. A new book about them just won two prizes at the NSW Literary Awards.

items.title: Monday Finance with Alan Kohler
items.summary: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

items.title: Rajesh Jiwan, 56, charged with murder after woman's body found in Hobart home
items.summary: A 56-year-old man has been charged with murder after the body of a woman was found in a Hobart residence over the weekend.

items.title: How WA's approach to shark mitigation has evolved over decades after another fatal attack
items.summary: The approach to tackling shark attacks has changed dramatically since the 2013 catch-and-kill policy, with a focus on shark tagging and tracking. But it's never enough to stop fatal attacks.

items.title: Free public transport, extended timetables among solutions to NSW live music scene, inquiry told
items.summary: An inquiry into the state of live music in NSW hears from industry stakeholders looking to service younger audiences sprawled across Greater Sydney.

items.title: Scott Pendlebury not looking too far into the future as AFL/VFL games record looms
items.summary: Collingwood champion Pendlebury will break the AFL games record against West Coast but hasn't pondered his future beyond then.

items.title: Innisfail man accused of paying to watch live videos of overseas child sex abuse
items.summary: Police allege the 38-year-old from the state's far north took part in more than 40 live shows on an online platform. WARNING: This story contains details of child sexual abuse.

items.title: Government blocks private colleges from offering new courses as part of student visa crackdown
items.summary: Australia suspends private colleges from applying to offer new courses to international students as part of a crackdown on abuse in the student visa system.

items.title: Australia's smelters are asking for more taxpayer funding to compete with China on critical minerals like antimony
items.summary: China is "playing a hard game" with its stranglehold on the global minerals and metals market and Australian smelters need taxpayer support to "play hard back".

items.title: Arsonist who started $1.5 million fire at Adelaide hotel site was motivated by 'retribution', court told
items.summary: The guilty man embarked on a two-week crime spree after his relationship soured with his business partner, who he also assaulted in front of the man's children.

items.title: SA premier plays down social media posts attempting to reveal his home address amid golf course controversy
items.summary: SA Premier Peter Malinauskas has played down social media posts attempting to reveal his home address in the aftermath of hundreds of trees being removed for the $45 million North Adelaide Golf Course.

items.title: Cull of 20,000 native birds at Newcastle Waters cattle station will not go ahead after parent company intervened
items.summary: A cull of 20,000 native birds on a Northern Territory cattle station will not be going ahead.

items.title: Anglo American sells central Queensland coal mines to UK company
items.summary: Anglo American says it has sold its five steelmaking coal mines in central Queensland to a UK mining company Dhilmar Ltd for up to $5.43 billion.

items.title: Trial begins, pitting pianist Jayson Gillham against Melbourne Symphony Orchestra over his Gaza comments
items.summary: Pianist Jayson Gillham is suing the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra in the Federal Court over political discrimination after comments he made about Gaza on stage during a 2024 performance.

items.title: Treasurer says 'death tax' claims are a scare campaign, 'pure and simple'
items.summary: The Coalition seizes on the federal government's broken tax promises, claiming a "death tax" has been sneaked into changes to trusts passed on through inheritance. The treasurer says that is a scare campaign.

items.title: Man jailed for assaulting off-duty police officer in Newtown has sentence reduced
items.summary: Koby Weaver, 21, was sentenced last month to two years' jail, after he ran into a brawl between two of his friends and two off-duty officers.

items.title: Make the most of cheap and versatile tinned beans with these recipes
items.summary: From vegetarian chilli to hearty soup, these easy recipes might convince you to add beans to your regular dinner rotation.

items.title: State of Origin: Why did Billy Slater leave Reece Walsh out of his Game I Queensland Maroons team?
items.summary: Reece Walsh's generational run to end 2025 was almost without peer, but he has rejoined the ranks of the mortals to start this year.

items.title: Vitrinite employees owed $16 million as Vulcan mine bids come due
items.summary: Federal Court documents show employees of the Vitrinite group of companies are collectively owed more than $16 million in entitlements. The mine near Dysart in Central Queensland has faced ongoing financial difficulties, exacerbated by lower coal prices.

items.title: Nicola Spurrier shares road safety message after she was hit by car while cycling
items.summary: SA public health chief Nicola Spurrier opens up about the moment when she was knocked from her bike by a car last year, and recounted the experience — which left her with broken bones and fearing for her life — only hours after a 39-year-old motorcyclist was killed in a crash in the Adelaide Hills.

items.title: Tasmanian polling shows One Nation would win a 'number of seats' in next state election
items.summary: A recent Tasmanian poll has shown that One Nation would win a "number of seats" in the next state election.

items.title: Federal treasurer orders more Chinese-linked investors to offload shares in Australian rare earths mine
items.summary: The federal treasurer orders another six companies to divest their holdings in Northern Minerals as concerns grow in Canberra that Chinese-backed investors are trying to seize control of the rare earths mining company.

items.title: Corbie Walpole, 25, appeals jail term for drunkenly setting friend on fire
items.summary: Corbie Walpole's legal team has argued the sentencing judge failed to properly consider psychiatric evidence about her mental health at the time of the offending.

items.title: Prime minister backs Whyalla steelmaking as work continues to bring blast furnace 'back to life'
items.summary: Anthony Albanese says he remains "very confident" in the future of steelmaking in Whyalla, and says the federal government is "continuing to work" with lead producer Nyrstar to secure its operations in Port Pirie and Hobart.

items.title: Tasmania Police investigating near-miss between truck and two motorcycles
items.summary: Police are investigating after dashcam footage emerged of a large truck narrowly avoiding hitting two motorcyclists on a rural road in Tasmania.

items.title: WA mouse plague pushes regulator to approve stronger bait
items.summary: Farm groups have welcomed the chemical regulator's long-awaited decision to approve double-strength mouse baits amid plague numbers in Western Australia and South Australia.

items.title: Parramatta City Council unveils plan for Chinatown in 'underutilised' part of Western Sydney
items.summary: The Parramatta City Council is planning a new Chinatown in an "under-utilised" part of the Western Sydney CBD, but community members want to see more than just food businesses.

items.title: Jonas Vingegaard claims second Giro d'Italia stage win, Jai Hindley fighting for podium spot
items.summary: Jai Hindley insists that the Giro d'Italia is not over despite Jonas Vinegagaard proving his mountain dominance with another impressive victory on stage nine.

items.title: Live frog found in bag of lettuce in remote Western Australian town
items.summary: A Kiwi couple in a remote port town on WA's south coast have shared an unlikely discovery, a live frog in a bag of Woolworths lettuce.

items.title: Court mention for 33-year-old charged with murder over Gooburrum death
items.summary: Yutthawichan Phetrchaisak is remanded in custody after being charged with the murder of a 42-year-old man north of Bundaberg.

items.title: Steven Miles squeaks a win in Stafford by-election, despite drop in Labor vote
items.summary: Labor candidate Luke Richmond will become the next member for Stafford, but the party's vote dropped several points.

items.title: Coffs Coast woman killed, three injured after crash at Karangi
items.summary: Police are investigating after a car flipped after crashing into a power pole.

items.title: Taiwanese president says island will not be sacrificed after Trump-Xi meeting
items.summary: Speaking after Donald Trump's meeting with China's president, Taiwan's leader says the island will not be sacrificed or traded and will not give up its free way of life.

items.title: Fashion designer Rochelle Gregory uses film to untangle her mental health battle
items.summary: Rochelle Gregory has clothed everyone from Paris Hilton to Dua Lipa. Now the designer is using film to candidly explore her mental health battle while showcasing her latest collection.

items.title: Albany hydrotherapy pool reopening rejected despite study finding it 'justifiable'
items.summary: The WA health minister rejects recommissioning Albany's public hydrotherapy pool despite a feasibility study saying there is a justifiable cause to do so.

items.title: 1.2 million Queensland homes unprepared as smoke alarm deadline nears
items.summary: A national company estimates many Queensland homes are unprepared as the deadline to install compliant smoke alarms nears.

items.title: No Walsh for Queensland as Blues coach accidentally lists former Maroon
items.summary: Billy Slater leaves Reece Walsh out of his squad for State of Origin I, while coach Laurie Daley accidentally announces the name of an ex-Queensland player.

items.title: New South Wales animal rescuers call for change amid a growing stray cat 'crisis'
items.summary: Animal shelters across New South Wales sound the alarm on a worsening stray cat crisis.

items.title: Central Australian Aboriginal ranger camp celebrates importance of women in protecting country
items.summary: Dozens of Central Land Council Aboriginal rangers gathered east of Alice Springs last week for the 2026 Women's Ranger Camp — a time for health, learning and healing in the wake of the town's recent loss of Kumanjayi Little Baby.

items.title: French probe into Jeffrey Epstein sees about 10 new alleged victims come forward
items.summary: About 10 new suspected victims have come forward as part of a French probe into the network of late US sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

items.title: Elk sighting in SA's south-east raises questions about deer farm escapes
items.summary: A South Australian landowner has spotted elk on her property, outside containment lines of deer farms. There are no known established wild elk herds in this state.

items.title: Commercial activity in Sydney's historic Callan Park one step closer as new foreshore plan revealed
items.summary: A new foreshore plan for Callan Park in Sydney's inner west includes a cafe, but the future of commercial opportunities at the site depends now on the passing of amended legislation.

items.title: Whyalla's steel and Port Pirie's lead are facing a make-or-break six months
items.summary: The South Australian government has put all its chips on the table for the Whyalla steelworks and Port Pirie lead smelter. The next six months will be critical.

items.title: Collingwood sobering-up centre to close less than four years after public intoxication reforms
items.summary: A 20-bed facility in the Melbourne suburb of Collingwood will close in 2027 after operating for just a few years following legal reforms.

items.title: Developers with state-tied projects donate to Queensland politicians after ban lifted
items.summary: More than $170,000 in donations from developers has been given to Queensland state politicians since laws banning developer donations were overturned in March.

items.title: Wallabies and All Blacks face similar dilemma when players head overseas
items.summary: Both Australia and New Zealand strive to protect Super Rugby Pacific as their premier domestic competition, even it if it has consequences for the Wallabies and All Blacks.

items.title: Ex-NSW Labor minister and NDIS campaigner says Albanese government can't rush scheme changes
items.summary: A former NSW Labor powerbroker and state disability minister pivotal in advocating for the establishment of the NDIS has warned against rushing through sweeping changes to the scheme.

items.title: Albanese and Taylor both looking to history for future of Australia's tax system
items.summary: The Albanese government and the opposition both want to turn back the clock on the Australian tax system.

items.title: Australia's interest rate rise comes as other countries maintain theirs. Why?
items.summary: As Australians pay more on their mortgages, some countries have had no rise in their interest rate in recent years. So what's going on?

items.title: Push for Australia to extract helium gas as Middle East war impacts global supply
items.summary: Helium is used in health care and to make microchips and semiconductors, but the Middle East war has affected supply. Some think it is an opportunity for Australia's gas industry.

items.title: UAE slams 'dangerous escalation' after drone hits near nuclear plant
items.summary: Abu Dhabi officials say radiation levels are normal and there was no damage to the plant, but authorities are now working to determine who carried out the attack.
